name: Tirzepatide
subtitle: Dual GIP/GLP-1 Receptor Agonist | Weight Loss & Diabetes

modifications: C20 fatty diacid conjugation for albumin binding

overview: Revolutionary dual receptor agonist FDA-approved for type 2 diabetes and chronic weight management. Demonstrates efficacy superior to single-mechanism alternatives with 15-22% body weight reduction in clinical trials. The first-in-class dual GIP/GLP-1 agonist provides enhanced metabolic benefits compared to GLP-1-only medications.
mechanism: Dual agonist targeting both GIP and GLP-1 receptors, producing glucose-dependent insulin stimulation, delayed gastric emptying, glucagon suppression, and central satiety signaling via hypothalamic pathways.
keyBenefits: Reduced cardiovascular risk (26% reduction in MACE)

items.name: Severe Obesity Management
items.description: Clinical trials demonstrate 15-22% body weight reduction in non-diabetic obese individuals, superior to existing weight loss medications.

items.name: Metabolic Syndrome Reversal
items.description: Improvements in waist circumference, blood pressure, triglycerides, HDL cholesterol, and insulin resistance markers.

items.name: Body Composition Optimization
items.description: Preferentially reduces visceral adipose tissue while preserving lean muscle mass with resistance training.

items.name: Type 2 Diabetes Management
items.description: Superior HbA1c reduction of 1.5-2.4% in clinical trials.

items.name: Insulin Resistance Improvement
items.description: Improves insulin sensitivity across diverse populations.

items.name: Beta Cell Preservation
items.description: May help preserve and restore pancreatic beta cell function.

items.name: MACE Reduction
items.description: 26% reduction in major adverse cardiovascular events demonstrated in SURPASS-CVOT trial.

items.name: Blood Pressure Reduction
items.description: Systolic and diastolic blood pressure reductions of 8-12 mmHg.

items.name: Lipid Profile Improvement
items.description: Triglyceride improvements of 20-30%, HDL enhancement, and apolipoprotein B reduction.

deliveryMethods.overview: Once-weekly subcutaneous injection. Can be taken any time of day, with or without food. Injection sites include thigh, abdomen (2+ inches from navel), or upper arm.

materials: Tirzepatide lyophilized powder vial (or pre-filled pen)
Insulin syringes (0.5ml or 1ml with fine needle)
steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature (15-20 minutes)
Clean vial tops with alcohol wipes, allow air drying
Draw bacteriostatic water carefully into syringe
Insert needle at 45-degree angle against glass wall
Inject water slowly down vial side to prevent foaming
Allow 2-3 minutes for clearing if cloudiness appears
Final solution must be completely clear and colorless

keyBenefits: Same day each week, any time of day

interactions.notes: Both are GLP-1 agonists—combining increases hypoglycemia and severe GI side effect risk.
Another GLP-1 agonist—dual therapy contraindicated due to additive effects.
May require significant insulin dose reduction due to improved sensitivity.
Complementary mechanisms for diabetes management and weight loss.
Growth hormone support may help preserve muscle mass during weight loss.
May help maintain metabolic rate and muscle preservation.
No known interactions, may support gut health and reduce GI effects.
NNMT inhibition may complement GLP-1 effects for metabolic optimization.

timeline.effects: Improved blood sugar control (diabetics), mild nausea common
Initial weight loss begins; GI side effects typically improve
1-3 lbs weight loss per week during active phase

stopSigns: Neck lumps, hoarseness, difficulty swallowing (thyroid concerns)
Severe hypoglycemic signs (confusion, sweating, rapid heartbeat)
contraindications: Personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma
monitoring: Start with lowest dose (2.5mg) and escalate every 4 weeks
May require diabetes medication adjustment to prevent hypoglycemia

good: White to off-white lyophilized powder without clumping
Intact vial seal with visible mg dosage labeling and batch numbers
Proper storage maintenance at 2-8°C, protected from light
warning: Compounded versions without proper quality control
bad: Powder clumping, discoloration, or yellow/brown appearance

references.title: Tirzepatide Once Weekly for the Treatment of Obesity (SURMOUNT-1)
references.keyFindings: Landmark phase 3 trial: 15mg weekly achieved 22.5% weight loss vs 2.4% placebo over 72 weeks. Weight reduction of ≥20% achieved in 57% of 15mg recipients.

references.title: Tirzepatide versus Semaglutide Once Weekly in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes (SURPASS-2)
references.keyFindings: Tirzepatide was noninferior and superior to semaglutide 1mg for HbA1c reduction and weight loss in type 2 diabetes. The 15mg dose achieved nearly twice the weight loss of semaglutide.

references.title: Tirzepatide once weekly for the treatment of obesity in people with type 2 diabetes (SURMOUNT-2)
references.keyFindings: 15mg dose achieved 15.7% weight loss over 72 weeks with significant cardiometabolic improvements. 79-83% of tirzepatide recipients achieved ≥5% weight loss vs 32% placebo.

references.title: Cardiovascular Outcomes with Tirzepatide versus Dulaglutide in Type 2 Diabetes (SURPASS-CVOT)
references.participants: 13,299 patients with T2DM and atherosclerotic CVD
references.keyFindings: Tirzepatide was noninferior to dulaglutide for MACE (composite of CV death, MI, or stroke). Several secondary endpoints favored tirzepatide, including reduced all-cause mortality after 4-year median follow-up.

references.title: Tirzepatide for Obesity Treatment and Diabetes Prevention (SURMOUNT-1 3-Year Extension)
references.keyFindings: Three years of tirzepatide resulted in sustained weight reduction of up to 19.7% (15mg) and 93% lower risk of progression to type 2 diabetes vs placebo (HR 0.07).

references.title: Tirzepatide for Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction and Obesity (SUMMIT)
references.keyFindings: Tirzepatide reduced the composite of CV death or worsening heart failure by 38% (HR 0.62) vs placebo over median 104 weeks. Improved health status scores and 6-minute walk distance.

references.title: Tirzepatide as Compared with Semaglutide for the Treatment of Obesity (SURMOUNT-5)
references.participants: Adults with obesity without type 2 diabetes
references.keyFindings: Head-to-head comparison: tirzepatide achieved 20.2% weight loss vs 13.7% with semaglutide at 72 weeks. Tirzepatide was superior for body weight and waist circumference reduction.

references.title: Tirzepatide for the Treatment of Obstructive Sleep Apnea and Obesity (SURMOUNT-OSA)
references.participants: Adults with moderate-to-severe OSA and obesity
references.keyFindings: Tirzepatide significantly reduced AHI with resolution of OSA in approximately 50% of participants. Also reduced body weight, hypoxic burden, hsCRP, and systolic blood pressure.

latestResearch.title: SURPASS-CVOT Results Published
latestResearch.summary: Landmark cardiovascular outcomes trial published in NEJM showing tirzepatide noninferior to dulaglutide for MACE in 13,299 patients with T2DM and atherosclerotic CVD, with favorable trends in secondary endpoints including all-cause mortality.

latestResearch.title: SURMOUNT-5 Head-to-Head vs Semaglutide
latestResearch.summary: First direct comparison published in NEJM demonstrating tirzepatide superior to semaglutide for weight loss (20.2% vs 13.7%) and waist circumference reduction at 72 weeks in adults with obesity.

latestResearch.title: 3-Year SURMOUNT-1 Extension for Diabetes Prevention
latestResearch.summary: Three-year data showing sustained weight loss up to 19.7% and 93% reduction in progression to type 2 diabetes in persons with obesity and prediabetes treated with tirzepatide.

latestResearch.title: SUMMIT Trial: Heart Failure Benefits
latestResearch.summary: Phase 3 SUMMIT trial showed tirzepatide reduced composite of CV death or worsening heart failure by 38% in patients with HFpEF and obesity, establishing cardiovascular benefits beyond weight loss.

quickStats.storage: Pen: 2-8°C before first use, room temp up to 21 days after. Compounded: 2-8°C
